channel.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
channel.description:
Yesterday we had a really interesting talk with [Carlos](https://codingcarlos.com/) from [Open Source Weekends](https://osweekends.com/) about the OSW community.
I think the main takeaways were: - They make sure people have fun at events. - They make sure people feel welcomed to the community. - They talk to everyone who joins and ask them what they want and how they can help. - If there's no conversation going on on a certain day, they start something to keep people engaged and active. - They have gathered together different OS communities that otherwise wouldn't have had the opportunity to collaborate (for example, data analysts with frontend devs). - They gave away a lot of stickers (for laptops) and t-shirts so people can recognize each other and feel they are part of something bigger. - They promoted the community giving talks at other events themselves.
Oh, and they have a beautiful slogan: "Peace, love and Open Source." :)

item.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.source: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends

item.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
That would be great, Carlos!

item.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
item.dc:creator: CodingCarlos
item.description:
Thanks for writing about us!

In June we will have another big event, so if you want to come and show Frontity to the OSW communnity, you’re more than invited

Read full topic
item.source: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends

item.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
Two weekends ago there were around 200 attendees to the OSW event at Campus, once we launch the beta we have to definitely present Frontity Framework there and spend the day with that community!

Read full topic
item.source: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends

item.title: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Yesterday we had a really interesting talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends about the OSW community.

I think the main takeaways were:

  • They make sure people have fun at events.
  • They make sure people feel welcomed to the community.
  • They talk to everyone who joins and ask them what they want and how they can help.
  • If there’s no conversation going on on a certain day, they start something to keep people engaged and active.
  • They have gathered together different OS communities that otherwise wouldn’t have had the opportunity to collaborate (for example, data analysts with frontend devs).
  • They gave away a lot of stickers (for laptops) and t-shirts so people can recognize each other and feel they are part of something bigger.
  • They promoted the community giving talks at other events themselves.

Oh, and they have a beautiful slogan: “Peace, love and Open Source.”

Read full topic
item.source: Our talk with Carlos from Open Source Weekends
